subject: Geology
title: Obsidian and Pumice
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Obsidian and Pumice.

hook.text: One is volcanic glass sharp enough for surgery. The other floats on water. Both came from the same process.

buildup.text: Obsidian forms when lava cools so fast that no crystals have time to grow. The result is smooth volcanic glass.

discovery.text: Pumice forms when gas-rich lava froths and cools. Trapped bubbles make it so light that it floats on water.

twist.text: Both are extrusive igneous rocks, but cooling speed and gas content create radically different textures.

climax.text: Obsidian and pumice show how the same magma can produce opposite results depending on conditions.

punchline.text: Volcanoes make both glass and foam.

quiz.question: Why does pumice float on water?
options: Trapped gas bubbles make it extremely light
It is made of organic material
